Title: The Arabidopsis Golgi-localized GDP-L-fucose transporter is required for plant development

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nucleotide sugar transporter (NST/TPT family), clade III; Golgi GDP-sugar transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed across organs and developmental stages; relatively high expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed across organs and developmental stages; relatively high expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: deoxyhexose sugar; cell wall and glycan monosaccharide
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Golgi-derived glycans including cell wall xyloglucan, rhamnogalacturonan II (RG-II), and N-glycans/glycoproteins
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for proper glycan fucosylation, normal plant growth and development, and correct organization of pectic polysaccharides such as RG-II
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Imports GDP-L-fucose into the Golgi lumen; GFT1 silencing causes strong reduction of L-fucose in cell wall polymers and glycoproteins, severe dwarfism, developmental arrest, and frequent lethality
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast heterologous expression and proteo-liposome transport assay with LC-MS/MS; kinetic transport analysis; transient YFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; RNAi knockdown in Arabidopsis; qPCR; cell wall monosaccharide profiling; OLIMP of xyloglucan; protein monosaccharide analysis; immunoblot of N-glycan fucose epitopes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GFT1 is a Golgi-localized nucleotide sugar transporter that preferentially transports GDP-Fuc in vitro with GMP as the exchange substrate. 2. GFT1 shows high-affinity GDP-Fuc transport (Km about 7 μM), whereas GONST1 has much weaker affinity for GDP-Fuc. 3. hpGFT1 knockdown plants show up to ~80% reduction in cell wall L-fucose, loss of fucosylated xyloglucan oligosaccharides, reduced glycoprotein/N-glycan fucosylation, severe dwarfism, and high seedling lethality. 4. The data support GFT1 as the major, likely sole, Golgi GDP-Fuc transporter required for Arabidopsis development.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard plant growth conditions; no special nutrient or stress treatment central to phenotype interpretation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi hairpin construct in pHANNIBAL/pART27; promoter not specified in provided text; YFP localization constructs used CaMV 35S promoter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for engineering glycan fucosylation in plant cell walls and glycoproteins, but strong suppression causes severe developmental defects and lethality, so partial/tissue-specific modulation would be required
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong knockdown causes severe dwarfism, abnormal development, and >50% early death; broad impairment of cell wall and protein fucosylation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport is highly specific for GDP-Fuc over GDP-Man and GDP-Glc in vitro; transport strictly depends on GMP exchange. GDP-Fuc pool size in planta was not reduced in knockdown lines, indicating defect is in Golgi import rather than biosynthesis. Gene was previously called GONST4 and renamed GFT1.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public microarray expression data: AtGenExpress developmental data set; no new deposition accession reported
